name: Kajabi Creator
reason: Creators build and sell courses, memberships, coaching products, and digital downloads. Kajabi is a subscription-based platform where creators keep ~100% of revenue (minus payment processing fees). Not an affiliate-first model.
payoutModel: Creators keep 100% of revenue. Payment processing fees apply: 2.9% + $0.30/transaction via Kajabi Payments. Additional plan-based transaction fees if using third-party processors (0.5%–5% depending on plan).
requirements: Paid subscription plan required (Kickstarter, Basic, Growth, Pro). Free trial available.
notes: Subscription plans range from ~$55–$319/month. No revenue share with Kajabi itself.

name: Coursera Instructor
reason: Instructors earn from courses/projects they create and teach. Revenue is earned by building content (Guided Projects, courses). Institutional partners negotiate revenue share directly.
payoutModel: Revenue share negotiated per partner/institution. For Guided Project Network instructors, Coursera pays a portion of subscription revenue. Institutional partners may receive flat fees or percentage of student revenue. Starting 2026, a 15% platform fee for course creators is expected.
requirements: Individual instructors can apply via Guided Project Network (teach.coursera.org). Full course creation requires institutional partnership (university or approved organization). Approval process required.
notes: Not fully open to independent individuals for full courses — primarily university/corporate partnerships. Guided Projects are the main route for independent instructors.

name: LinkedIn Learning Instructor
reason: Instructors earn from courses they record and publish on LinkedIn Learning. Earn upfront fee plus ongoing royalties based on viewership/engagement.
payoutModel: Upfront fee: $1,000–$5,000 per course. Ongoing royalties: 10%–15% of revenue from subscribers who view the content. Performance-based royalties.
requirements: Application and approval required. LinkedIn looks for recognized subject matter experts in business, tech, leadership, or creative fields. Strong LinkedIn presence and professional experience expected.
notes: Not open to everyone — competitive selection process. Instructors are vetted by LinkedIn.

name: Thinkific Creator
reason: Creators build and sell courses directly on Thinkific. Platform charges subscription fees, not revenue cuts. Creators earn from content they create.
payoutModel: Creators keep 100% of course revenue. Transaction surcharges apply only when using third-party payment processors (Stripe/PayPal): 1%–5% depending on plan. No surcharge with Thinkific Payments.
requirements: Paid or free plan subscription. Free plan available with limited features. Paid plans from ~$36–$149/month.
notes: GMV of $459.1M in 2024, growing platform. Strong creator-first model.

name: Society6 Artist
reason: Society6 is currently NOT accepting new artist applications. Their help page states: 'We're not accepting new applications right now.' Platform has also moved to a curated model as of March 2025, which makes it even more restrictive.
payoutModel: When open: 10% markup on select products (pillows, wall tapestries, framed art); 5% markup on all other products. Payments via PayPal monthly.
requirements: Applications closed as of 2025. Previously open to all artists.
notes: Was a creator program (artists earn from uploaded art on print-on-demand products). Currently INACTIVE for new signups. Mark REJECTED due to not accepting new creators.

name: Zazzle Designer
reason: Designers create and upload original designs that get printed on products. Earn royalties from product sales — a clear creator-earning model.
payoutModel: Designers set their own royalty rate: 5%–50% of base price for physical products (as of April 2025 cap); 5%–99% for instant downloads. Marketing Royalty Fee deducted (35%–50% of gross royalty) for sales driven by Zazzle marketing. Excess Royalty Fee of 5% if royalty rate >10%.
requirements: Free to join. Create a Zazzle store and upload designs. No follower requirements.
notes: Recent 2025 changes capped physical product royalties at 50% (down from 99%). Marketing fees can significantly cut earnings. Effective rates for most sellers: 10%–15% of base price recommended. Still active and open to new designers.

name: Dribbble Marketplace
reason: Designers earn by selling digital assets (templates, fonts, icons, UI kits, illustrations) through Dribbble's Marketplace (powered by Creative Market). Earning is based on content created, not referrals.
payoutModel: Creative Market charges ~50% commission on sales. Sellers keep ~50% of sale price. Payouts via ACH/eCheck or PayPal monthly, minimum $20 balance.
requirements: Dribbble Pro membership recommended (provides express shop setup). Must apply to open a Creative Market shop — portfolio review required. Recommended to have 5–10 products ready.
notes: Dribbble acquired Creative Market; marketplace is jointly powered by both platforms. Sellers must create a Creative Market shop to sell.
